claim: Avoid provider loyalty; use multiple models in ANDs not ORs: Gemini for computer use, OpenAI Realtime API for orchestration, Claude Code for implementation
actionable: Build multi-agent systems that leverage best-in-class tools from different providers; do not default to single-vendor solutions
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Multi-agent observability via Claude Code hooks enables live visibility into all agent activities without being stuck in the loop
actionable: Use Claude Code hooks to instrument all agents; stream live summaries of agent actions (via cheap models) to observability UI; only dive deep when issues occur
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Agents need closed-loop validation: Cloud Code agent performs work, Gemini 2.5 Computer Use validates in browser, feedback loop completes
actionable: Add browser validation agents (Gemini Computer Use) as a tool within Claude Code; use success/failure feedback to trigger corrections
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Voice-based agent orchestration via OpenAI Realtime API is practical now; agents can be controlled entirely through voice
actionable: Use OpenAI Realtime API to build voice orchestrator agents; orchestrator manages multiple Claude Code agents via natural language commands
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Agent input layers must accept both voice and text; output layers must loop back to inputs; this closes the feedback loop
actionable: Design multi-agent systems with flexible input/output: voice from user → orchestrator → coding agents → browser validation → output (audio/text/files) → loops back
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Custom slash commands in Claude Code (like /plan, /build) compress workflows into single natural language prompts for agents
actionable: Define custom slash commands that encapsulate reusable workflows; agents can invoke them naturally without needing to remember implementation details
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Orchestrator agent layer should be thin and flexible: CRUD operations on any agent type (not hardcoded to specific ones)
actionable: Design orchestrator with generic create/list/command agents tools; make agent pool pluggable so you can swap models/types later without rewriting orchestrator
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Agent interference risks arise when boundaries are unclear: separate backend and frontend agents but enforce non-overlapping responsibilities
actionable: When splitting agents by task, be explicit in prompts about which systems each agent owns; monitor observability for overlapping modifications
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Scaling impact requires scaling compute: direct causal relationship between compute usage and engineering output
actionable: Do not optimize for fewer agent calls; optimize for outcome; use more agents and more compute to unlock higher impact
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code

claim: Tactical agentic coding course teaches: build systems that build systems; agents should orchestrate other agents, not just write code directly
actionable: Layer your agents: orchestrator → builder agents → validation agents; each layer operates on outputs of previous layer
source_title: BIG 3 SUPER AGENT: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use, OpenAI Realtime API, Claude Code
